title: No `stdout` output when building an image with `podman` 2.1.1
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When building from the `Dockerfile` provided below, `docker` prints the output of `apt-get` to `stdout`, while `podman` (2.1.1) does not.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create the following `Dockerfile`:

        FROM docker.io/rocker/tidyverse:3.6.3
        RUN \
            apt-get update && \
            apt-get install --assume-yes --no-install-recommends \
            git-lfs gnupg ssh-client

2. Build using `podman build -t test .`

**Describe the results you received:**
The image finishes building - without any output provided by `apt-get`.

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect to see the `stdout` output from `apt-get` (as is the case when using `docker`).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14.9
Built:        Wed Sep 30 21:31:11 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  swapFree: 896266240

  uptime: 2h 17m 39.55s (Approximately 0.08 days)

        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.1.1-7.fc32.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
`podman` is running in a XEN VM (as provided by QubesOS).

comments.body:
It works for me with 2.1.1 and master.
```
$ printf 'FROM alpine\nRUN 1>&2 echo msg to STDERR\n' | podman build --no-cache -t t - > /dev/null
msg to STDERR
```
Maybe the STEP is using the cache. It would look like this:
```
STEP 2: RUN 1>&2 echo msg to STDERR
--> Using cache 36401f7fc6817382180c8bc12846a833194f2a1334ca7d0ea204da37d5e23d33
```

That works for me as well (even with the tidyverse image:

    STEP 1: FROM docker.io/rocker/tidyverse:3.6.3
    STEP 2: RUN     1>&2 echo msg to STDERR
    msg to STDERR
    STEP 3: COMMIT test
    --> e88140fe4e1
    e88140fe4e14afb0a8403148d386b55699ebf89cac314d0653b7a521b612503b

but none of the `apt-get` output I see with `docker` gets produced, so debugging is difficult ... where does that output go?

Your exact reproducer works for me:

Can you try this simple Dockerfile:
```
FROM debian
RUN apt-get update && \
         apt-get install --assume-yes --no-install-recommends git
```

That minimal version runs through, but gives me NOT apt output

```
$ podman build --no-cache -t test .
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/rocker/tidyverse:3.6.3
STEP 2: MAINTAINER Johannes Graumann (johannes.graumann@mpi-bn.mpg.de)
--> b5633728208
STEP 3: RUN     apt-get update &&     apt-get install --assume-yes --no-install-recommends git
--> bcea93ac2de
STEP 4: ENV     ROOT=TRUE     DISABLE_AUTH=TRUE
--> f0b118f8e96
STEP 5: COPY ./user-settings /home/rstudio/.rstudio/monitored/user-settings/user-settings
STEP 6: COMMIT test
--> c5d0f0f111a
c5d0f0f111a687c643b69396d27155f70cfeb5534557a29a1d6b6340aadae4fd
```
Could you test if it works with `buildah bud`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
No response within a month, closing reopen if new data comes in.
